The Azalea plant is a flowering shrub that belongs to the Rhododendron genus, known for its vibrant colors and fragrant blooms. The Azalea plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of the Azalea plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Azalea is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
